quote_of_day.text: Having a sacred animal is the most retarded thing a culture could do. Eating beef is based, it's just a fucking animal. If you think it's a god get your malnutritioned brain checked. >muh milk Every other mammal gives milk, why is cow special.
quote_of_day.name: Ambedkar

We need more anons like iac to make cool shit. Im a very impressed pajeet. Writing this to see if it will get featured on the newspaper or not, im not sure how he set it up but if the !thing works then hello indus news! Im on da news niggaaaaaa
guest_letters.name: Narthie

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of cultural critique moved into the board this morning, bringing scattered showers of dissent. Anon Criticizes Sacred Animal Concept, Sparking Debate, with one anon dismissing sacred animals as 'retarded' and sparking a debate that.

A weak front of self-promotion pushed through the Discourse section, as Ambedkar attempted to shill the site on social media, garnering some interest.

Meanwhile, a user's plea for motivation stalled, overcome by a drizzle of personal struggles and self-doubt. offering varying degrees of support and derailment.

A brief cold snap of moderation warnings swept through Notices, as Fuwawa cautioned against engaging with spammers, and an anon shared AI-generated saree images, a fleeting but notable clearing in the discussion fog.

articles.title: Anon Criticizes Sacred Animal Concept, Sparking Debate
articles.body:
Ambedkar opened the thread by criticizing the concept of sacred animals in cultures, specifically targeting the reverence for cows.

He argued that having a sacred animal is 'the most retarded thing a culture could do' and that eating beef is 'based,' calling it 'just a fucking animal.' Anon responded by suggesting that the disagreement stems from different frameworks and moral layers that people assume are relevant to each other.

They mentioned that even if one doesn't care about animal welfare, the spiritual layer of beliefs exists independently. Another anon clarified that cows are not considered God, but rather a protected animal in the context of Aghniya.

They explained that the concept of protecting cows is rooted in their sacred status, but acknowledged that some cows have mixed with other breeds and are sometimes killed.

The discussion also touched on genetics, with an anon correcting the myth that the Y chromosome is weaker than the X chromosome. They explained that both chromosomes are fundamentally the same and evolved differently in mammals.

Narthie later expressed frustration with the thread, stating that it was initially decent but was ruined by a few users, including the OP, who they called 'retards.' Anon also discussed the idea of starting a new branch of faith, with one user suggesting that they could create a new branch of the dharmic tree.

Anon expressed indifference to people starting their own faith as long as they don't ask others to change. The conversation also involved users like Haggu and Soythie, who made comments on genetics and the ease of offending different religious groups.

Nikhil Fuentes shared a personal experience of feeling 'unblock completely' after adopting a vegan lifestyle, which they claimed helped them with their mental state and 'quantum entangled' feelings.

articles.title: Ambedkar pushes site promotion on social media
articles.body:
Ambedkar kicked off the discussion by suggesting that the site needs more traffic and should be promoted on Instagram and Reddit.

Ambedkar himself acknowledged that low-level design issues and moderation were more pressing concerns than promotion, but expressed a desire to improve the site's UI and reward quality posters.

Another poster, Soyjeet, weighed in on the discussion, criticising both Instagram and Reddit users, labelling them as 'retarded' and 'clout-hungry whore spawns'.

A later suggestion was made by Soythie, proposing that someone should pay for Google ads to promote the site, while Gura responded with a remark about old users being tired of new users falling for 'hagfish bait'.

Ambedkar returned to the topic of promotion, suggesting that search engine optimisation (SEO) could help the site appear in search results. He also mentioned that the site was already visible on Google's search engine.

Other users, including Yui and Nepajeet, chimed in with their thoughts on the site's users and the challenges of moderation.

Ambedkar continued to engage with the discussion, addressing issues such as spoiler images on the homepage and a new CAPTCHA system.

articles.title: User seeks motivation, gets derailed
articles.body:
Ambedkar opened the thread saying his dreams were being crushed every day and his mind and body were sabotaging him. He asked for motivational pyaaris to help him push his limits.

Ozymandias and Fuwawa responded, but their replies quickly derailed from motivation. Ozymandias joked about names and Fuwawa made a crude comment about someone's belly.

Ambedkar himself contributed to the derailment, making a comment about flat girls with big butts. The conversation continued to deteriorate, with Fuwawa and Ozymandias exchanging jokes and insults.

Fuwawa repeatedly brought up the topic of 'Rumia', calling her cute, and Ozymandias teased Fuwawa about his reactions. Yui joined in, making a comment about Ambedkar liking lolis and another about fubuki.

Meanwhile, Mococo posted a string of enthusiastic characters. Throughout the thread, Ambedkar's original request for motivation was largely ignored, and the conversation devolved into absurd exchanges.

articles.title: Fuwawa warns against responding to spammer
articles.body:
Fuwawa asked others not to reply to a spammer from /r9k/, calling the spammer a "madarchod" who doesn't understand the board. Fuwawa suggested ignoring the spammer so the thread would auto-delete.

Nepajeet translated Fuwawa's original post, saying the spammer was from /r9k/ and didn't understand the board.

Motivanon and others responded, with Motivanon calling the spammer's tactic "genius." The conversation included various brief exchanges, with Dyaush mentioning local dialects and Soythie asking about IP changes.

articles.title: Anon Creates AI-Generated Saree Images
articles.body: Aizen sparked a discussion with a post that drew criticism. Replies included accusations of wasting potential. Nepajeet responded with a link to saree images, claiming they were created by an anon who had their threads deleted from another board. Others criticized Aizen's content, calling it uninteresting or not good enough. Some posters suggested Aizen could create better content, like AI-generated videos, while others made jokes or personal attacks.
